Easy.q: What is the holy book of Christianity?
Easy.hint: It is split into the Old Testament and the New Testament.

Easy.q: What name is given to the holy month of fasting in Islam?
Easy.hint: Healthy adults abstain from food and drink from dawn until sunset during this month.

Easy.q: What term describes the belief in exactly one single God?

Medium.q: What is the Christian belief in the Trinity?
options: God exists as three distinct persons: Father, Son, and Holy Spirit
There are three separate Gods in heaven
The Bible was written by three prophets
The three main branches of the Church
Medium.hint: They are co-equal and co-eternal, yet form one single divine being.

Medium.q: Which of the Five Pillars of Islam requires giving 2.5% of savings to charity each year?
Medium.hint: It is a form of wealth purification and social justice.

Medium.q: What ethical concept means treating people fairly and equally, regardless of their background?
Medium.hint: It is often symbolized by a balanced set of scales.

Medium.q: In Christianity, what sacrament commemorates the Last Supper through sharing bread and wine?
Medium.hint: It is also known as Mass or Divine Liturgy.

Medium.q: What is the primary meaning of 'Jihad' in Islam?
options: To go on a long pilgrimage route
To struggle or strive in the way of Allah
To copy out holy texts by hand
Medium.hint: The 'Greater' form is an internal spiritual struggle against sin and temptation.

Hard.q: What is the 'Problem of Evil' in religious philosophy?
options: How an all-powerful, all-loving God can allow suffering to exist
The origin of criminal laws in society
A debate over the existence of heaven
Hard.hint: It questions the compatibility of an omnipotent, omnibenevolent creator with a world full of pain.

Hard.q: Which theory of punishment focuses on changing a criminal's behavior so they can re-enter society as a good citizen?
Hard.hint: It aims to reform or rehabilitate the offender.

Hard.q: What Christian concept is defined as God's unconditional love and unmerited favor toward humanity?
Hard.hint: It is a free gift from God that offers salvation from sin.

Hard.q: What do Muslims believe happens during 'Laylat al-Qadr' (The Night of Power)?
options: The creation of the universe was completed
The Quran was first revealed to Prophet Muhammad by Angel Jibril
The migration from Mecca to Medina occurred
The clearing of idols from the Kaaba
Hard.hint: It occurs during the last ten days of Ramadan and is considered the holiest night of the year.

Hard.q: What form of pacifism states that violence is absolutely wrong in every single circumstance with no exceptions?
Hard.hint: An follower of this stance would refuse to fight, even in self-defense or during a just war.

Hard.q: What is the meaning of the concept 'Sanctity of Life'?
options: Life is valuable only if a person is happy
All life is sacred and holy because it is created and blessed by God
Humans have complete ownership over when life starts and ends
Only religious leaders have valuable lives
Hard.hint: This principle is central to religious arguments against abortion and euthanasia.

Hard.q: What are the two main branches of Islam that emerged after a disagreement over leadership succession?
Hard.hint: The division originated over who should succeed the Prophet Muhammad as leader of the Muslim community.

Hard.q: What type of love does Christianity advocate for, defined as selfless, sacrificial, and universal Christian love?
Hard.hint: It is the type of love Jesus demonstrated on the cross and commanded his followers to show to everyone, including enemies.

Challenger.q: Which cosmological argument for the existence of God was proposed by Thomas Aquinas, asserting that everything has a starting cause?
Challenger.hint: It states that the universe cannot have an infinite chain of causes, so there must be a starting point that set it off.

Challenger.q: What does the Islamic term 'Tawhid' explicitly declare?
options: The daily obligation to perform 5 structured prayers
The absolute oneness and unity of Allah
Challenger.hint: It is the core belief of Islam, stated clearly in the Shahadah.

Challenger.q: In Christian theology, what does the term 'Atonement' mean?
options: The process of creating human beings from dust
The reconciliation of humanity with God through the sacrificial death and resurrection of Jesus
The physical ascension of saints into heaven
Challenger.hint: It is about making amends for human sin, bringing people back into 'at-one-ment' with God.

Challenger.q: What historical Christian declaration outlines the core beliefs about the Trinity, creation, and salvation?
Challenger.hint: It was compiled at early ecumenical councils to establish clear Christian doctrine.

Challenger.q: What condition of the 'Just War Theory' states that war must only be declared by an official government or ruler?
Challenger.hint: Private individuals or rebel groups cannot legally launch a just war under this rule.

Challenger.q: What do Muslims believe the term 'Risalah' refers to?
options: The system of communication from Allah via prophets and holy books
The predestination of all human choices
The structural rules governing a caliphate
Challenger.hint: It represents channel of guidance or divine messages sent to humanity throughout history.

Challenger.q: What moral philosophy evaluates an action based on whether it fulfills a duty or follows a fixed moral rule, regardless of the consequences?
Challenger.hint: Kant's Categorical Imperative is a famous example of this rule-based ethical system.

Challenger.q: What is the primary role of the 'Ummah' in Islamic lifestyle tracking?
options: The global brotherhood and community of all Muslims
The management of local mosque property
The judicial court system enforcing religious laws
The direct lineage of the Prophet
Challenger.hint: It binds all Muslims together across different nations and cultures.

Challenger.q: Which teleological argument uses the complexity of an eye or a watch to argue for an intelligent creator?
Challenger.hint: If you found a watch on a heath, you would assume a watchmaker designed it, not that it appeared by chance.

Challenger.q: What does the Christian concept of 'Stewardship' mean regarding the environment?
options: Humans have absolute right to exploit natural resources for profit
Humans are caretakers appointed by God to look after and protect the earth
The earth will destroy itself automatically
Nature holds more power than the creator
Challenger.hint: It comes from the command in Genesis to tend and care for the Garden of Eden.

Challenger.q: What Islamic concept deals with Al-Qadr?
options: The regular cycle of washing before prayer
Predestination; the belief that Allah knows and determines everything that happens
The design of geometric art patterns
The collection of stories about the life of Muhammad
Challenger.hint: It is one of the Six Articles of Faith in Sunni Islam, exploring the relationship between divine control and human free will.

Challenger.q: What is the focus of Christian 'Situation Ethics', popularized by Joseph Fletcher?
options: Following absolute laws from the Bible in every case
Doing the most loving thing in each individual situation based on agape love
Evaluating actions based on how much money they save
Challenger.hint: It rejects rigid rules and states that love is the only absolute standard.

Challenger.q: What are the names of the two angels who Muslims believe record every single human action for the Day of Judgment?
Challenger.hint: They sit on a person's shoulders, writing down good and bad deeds.

Challenger.q: What group within the early Christian community used peaceful resistance and refused to fight in the Roman army?
Challenger.hint: They followed Jesus' commands to 'turn the other cheek' and 'love your enemies' literally.

Challenger.q: What does the term 'Pluralism' mean regarding religious beliefs in society?
options: The view that only one religion holds absolute truth
The view that all religions are equally valid pathways to God and truth
The complete rejection of all religious ideas by the state
A system where state laws are written by bishops
Challenger.hint: It welcomes religious diversity, believing that no single religion has an exclusive monopoly on truth.
